entries.description: Slot placeholder background colors in the design tree (e.g., #CFF7D3 on footer title slot, #EBFFEE on footer link slot, #E6E6E6 on hero text content) appear as visible colored boxes in the HTML rendering. These colors likely represent design-system slot areas and may not be intended as visible backgrounds in the final design. The design tree faithfully reproduces them, but they cause visual discrepancies.

The Panel Image Double section has opacity:0.8 on the outer container, which in HTML applies to all child elements (images, text). Figma's opacity may only apply to the background fill. This distinction is not communicated in the design tree and required interpretation.

Card heights in Card Grid Image are determined by flex-wrap behavior with min-width constraints. The design tree shows cards as 325x357 each in rows of 3, but without explicit grid/column count guidance, a developer must calculate the 3-column layout from section width, padding, and gap values.

The root component is set to a fixed 375px width, causing the design to not adapt to wider viewports. At 768px, the design stays narrow in the center rather than filling the available width. This is a fundamental responsive design issue: the root frame has a hardcoded width rather than using 100% width with a max-width constraint.

The design has slot background colors (CFF7D3, EBFFEE) on footer slot areas that appear to be design scaffolding/debug colors rather than intended production colors. These are visible in the Figma screenshot and must be reproduced but cause confusion about design intent.

The root component has a fixed 1200px width with no responsive breakpoints defined, but a 1920px screenshot exists suggesting the design should expand. Without size constraints or responsive tokens, the expanded viewport conversion requires inference beyond available data.

The Section node is marked as content-image but also has layout properties (row-gap: 10px, flex-direction: column) suggesting it may have had children that weren't included in the design tree. The fixture shows a placeholder graphic centered in the area.

The Panel Image Double uses opacity: 0.8 on the entire section including images, resulting in the images appearing slightly transparent/faded. This is unusual — typically opacity would apply to a background overlay, not the whole section with images.

The design tree shows the Section node has both a solid fill background (#E3E3E3) AND an image fill (opacity: 0.2 with imageRef), but the design tree only emits content-image: url(images/section@2x.png) — it's unclear whether the image is a background overlay or the primary content. The FIT scale mode with 0.2 opacity suggests a subtle background, but the design tree presents it as a content-image.

The Footer 'Title' and 'Links' SLOT nodes have debug-colored backgrounds (CFF7D3 green, EBFFEE light-green) that appear as visible colored regions in both Figma and rendered output. These are clearly design system placeholder colors that a developer might assume are accidental, but they're intentional visual markers from the Figma design.
